title: DungeonQuest - Review - Top 10 Game as of 2020
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7dlhZLngdvM

Daniel (@dungeondive) delivers a thorough review of Dungeon Quest, the iconic Games Workshop dungeon crawl that epitomizes sword-and-sorcery adventure gaming. This is a game about pure risk, danger, and the slim 15% chance of survival. You pick a hero, choose your starting tower, and race against a 26-turn day cycle to plunder treasure and escape the castle alive. The genius lies in its elegant simplicity: randomly drawn room cards create encounters with monsters, traps, and dead ends, all resolved with delightfully quick mechanics.

Daniel emphasizes how Dungeon Quest succeeds because it captures the essence of heroic fantasy better than far more complicated games. The pressure-luck mechanic—choosing when to push deeper into danger or retreat with your gains—perfectly mirrors the high-stakes decisions that define sword-and-sorcery tales. The Catacombs expansion adds fun variations, while the hero expansions, though introducing extra rules, can slightly dilute that beautiful simplicity Daniel treasures most. Even the "curse of the wizard" rotating the corridors becomes a legendary table joke.

----

Beyond the mechanics, what makes Dungeon Quest's speed and simplicity more thrilling than complex dungeon crawls that take hours to complete?
